pattern: Rather than rescan the long string per word, advance all words together: bucket each word by its next-needed character and, sweeping the long string once, promote the waiting words whenever their awaited character arrives.
hints: Checking each word independently rescans the long string repeatedly; instead think about all words progressing as you read the long string a single time.
Group every word by the next character it is waiting for; when you read a character, every word waiting on it advances to wait on its following character.

Keep 26 buckets of (word, position) waiters; for each char of the long string, drain its bucket and re-file each waiter under its next character, counting a word the moment its position reaches its length.

edge_inputs.description: three of four words are subsequences
words longer than s or out of order do not match
single character string and single character words
single-letter and two-letter words, some ordered wrongly
word needs more copies of a letter than s provides

stress.description: random long string and many random short words over a small alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:6]
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    w = rng.randint(1, 200)
    words = []
    for _ in range(w):
        ln = rng.randint(1, 8)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(ln)))
    return [s, words]
stress.note: small alphabet makes many words match, stressing the bucketing
